text: Aerial Sentinels on the open road. No cover. You hear them before you see the shadow.
Harvest Town is visible from the highway. Smoke, gardens, people moving in orderly lines. Jerome is very quiet for a long time.
A tire blows on the interstate. No spares left. You're on foot for six hours before finding a replacement.
An overturned supply convoy — the robots abandoned it mid-route when something called them off. Jerome reads it as grace. Maya reads it as opportunity.
A plains storm with no warning. Two hours of hail. The tarp holds but the food pack doesn't.
Jerome gathers everyone at dusk. He doesn't quote scripture tonight. He just talks about why he's still here. It helps.
The plains are so flat you can see the stars unbroken to the horizon. It's the kind of beautiful that makes you ache.
